methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $11M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 9 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Furno Materials is a seed-stage climate and energy technology company that has raised $11M to date. The valuation landscape for early-stage companies in this sector is sparse: while nine private peers operate in Climate & Energy (including Helion, Redwood Materials, QuantumScape, KoBold Metals, and Electric Hydrogen), and one public comparable exists (LNZA), the narrow peer set at seed stage and similar valuation tier precludes reliable median benchmarking.

Given this constraint, stage-progression analysis provides the primary valuation signal. Using $11M in total raised as a proxy for current implied value and applying a typical 2.5x step-up multiplier from seed to next stage yields $28M. This method is grounded in observed venture progression norms rather than sector-specific comps. A sensitivity range of ±15% around this point estimate generates a low of $23M and a high of $32M.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $23M to $32M, centered on $28M. The primary uncertainty is the absence of direct peer comparables at Furno's stage and sector specificity; this range rests on stage-progression mechanics rather than traded or recent private financing benchmarks. Validation would require either additional seed-stage climate tech transactions or updated comparable financings from the identified peer set.
